sessions.intent: Design Data envelope architecture — Engine.Types, Type context, Data restructure, envelope pipeline

before.assumptions: Type knowledge scattered across three static/instance sources. Data has no context. Type is eagerly derived. No transport serialization view exists.
before.risk: Large architectural change touching Data (universal type), Type, and TypeMapping. Phased approach mitigates — each phase independently shippable.

sessions.plan: Four-phase design: (1) Engine.Types replaces static TypeMapping, (2) Type gets context + lazy derivation, Data gets late-bound context, (3) Data partial class split + Out view, (4) Envelope pipeline with Wrap/Compress/Encrypt chain. OBP throughout.

after.status: Design complete. Four-phase plan ready for coder handoff.
after.health: Good. No code changes, pure design.
after.notes: Coder should read plan.md + learnings.md before starting Phase 1.

sessions.intent: Implement Phase 1: Engine.Types — consolidate scattered type knowledge into a single live instance on Engine

before.assumptions: Type knowledge is scattered across static TypeMapping (Engine/Utility), FileModule.TypeMapping (instance), and MimeTypeHelper (Utils). Data.cs, file/types.cs, and StepActions reference static TypeMapping directly.
before.risk: Phase 1 is additive — no existing behavior changes. Risk is low.

sessions.plan: Create PLang/Runtime2/Engine/Types/this.cs with all type knowledge. Add Types property to Engine. Add EngineTypes global alias. Write 62 TUnit tests. Keep old static TypeMapping as-is until Phase 2.

actions.context: New @this class consolidating PLang name<->CLR type, extension->Kind, extension->MIME, Kind->compressibility.
Added Types property to Engine, EngineTypes global alias.
62 TUnit tests covering all public methods.

after.status: Phase 1 complete. Engine.Types created with full type knowledge. 62 tests pass.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v1

before.assumptions: 62 tests for new class, likely happy path only.

sessions.plan: Run tests, analyze for intent vs implementation, check edge cases.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v2 — Phase 2 context + lazy derivation

before.assumptions: v1 findings likely not addressed yet.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify v1 carry-forward, analyze Phase 2 quality.

sessions.intent: Verify v2 fixes + Phase 3 test quality

before.assumptions: Coder fixed v2 findings and implemented Phase 3.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify fixes, analyze Phase 3.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of Phase 4 envelope pipeline

before.assumptions: Pipeline involves serialization and compression — failure modes at transport boundary.

sessions.plan: Analyze pipeline tests, check error paths.

actions.context: Critical: Decompress no exception handling. Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: Needs fixes. 1366 tests pass but Decompress will crash on corrupt data.
Approved. 1372 tests pass. Ready for auditor.

sessions.intent: Full code review of data-envelope-architecture Phases 1-4

before.assumptions: Tester v5 approved, 1372 tests pass. Code should be functionally correct. Review focuses on OBP compliance, thread safety, contract fidelity, and ripple impact.
before.risk: Data is the universal type — any issue in Data.cs affects every module. Engine.Types is shared singleton — thread safety is critical.

sessions.plan: Read all production code and tests. Verify tests pass independently. Check OBP compliance, thread safety, error handling conventions, serialization correctness. Write auditor-report.json with numbered findings.

actions.context: Full review of all changed production files. Found 8 findings: 2 major (thread safety, temporal coupling), 3 minor (ServiceError, double-navigation, ContainsValue), 3 nit.
Test quality verified independently. 1372 pass. Adequate coverage for all phases. Gap: error key assertions after ServiceError fix.
8 findings written. Approved with fixes recommended.

after.status: Review complete. 8 findings in auditor-report.json. Approved with fixes recommended.
after.health: Good overall. Thread safety (finding #1) is the priority fix. Code is clean, well-structured, and OBP-compliant.
after.notes: Two major findings should be addressed before merge. Thread safety in Engine.Types is latent but becomes real with concurrent contexts. RehydrateNestedData temporal coupling is fragile but functional.

sessions.intent: Verify auditor fix commit + deep test quality analysis of fixes themselves

before.assumptions: Coder fixed all 8 auditor findings. Code changes are correct structurally. Question is whether the fixes themselves have test coverage.
before.risk: Safety features (zip bomb, thread safety) added without regression tests.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite, verify each auditor fix in code, analyze whether new code paths are tested, identify false greens where fixes exist in code but not in tests.

actions.context: Verified all 8 auditor fixes correct in code. Found 1 critical gap (zip bomb untested), 1 major gap (thread safety untested), 2 minor gaps (StatusCode, inner context). Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: Review complete. 4 findings in test-report.json. Verdict: needs-fixes.
after.health: Code is correct but two security-critical features lack regression tests.
after.notes: Critical: zip bomb protection (MaxDecompressedSize) has zero test coverage. Major: ConcurrentDictionary/lock has no concurrent test.
